questions.question: A major chord is made up of which intervals?
questions.explanation: A major chord = root + major 3rd (4 semitones) + perfect 5th (7 semitones).

questions.question: What notes make up a C major chord?
questions.explanation: C major: C (root), E (major 3rd), G (perfect 5th).

questions.question: How does a minor chord differ from a major chord?
options: The 3rd is lowered by a half step
questions.correct_answer: The 3rd is lowered by a half step
questions.explanation: Minor chord = major chord with a flattened 3rd. C minor = C, E♭, G.

questions.question: What notes make up a G major chord?
questions.explanation: G major: G (root), B (major 3rd), D (perfect 5th).

questions.question: What is a triad?
options: A three-note chord built in thirds
questions.correct_answer: A three-note chord built in thirds
questions.explanation: A triad consists of three notes stacked in intervals of thirds.

questions.question: What notes make up an A minor chord?
questions.explanation: A minor: A (root), C (minor 3rd), E (perfect 5th).

questions.question: What is a diminished triad?
questions.explanation: Diminished = minor 3rd + diminished 5th (6 semitones from root).

questions.question: What is an augmented triad?
questions.explanation: Augmented = major 3rd + augmented 5th (8 semitones from root).

questions.question: What is the I-IV-V-I chord progression in C major?
questions.explanation: In C major: I = C, IV = F, V = G. So the progression is C-F-G-C.

questions.question: What does a '7' mean after a chord name (e.g., G7)?
options: Add the 7th scale degree to the triad
questions.correct_answer: Add the 7th scale degree to the triad
questions.explanation: G7 = G, B, D, F — a G major triad with an added minor 7th (F).

questions.question: What is a chord inversion?
options: Rearranging which note is in the bass
questions.correct_answer: Rearranging which note is in the bass
questions.explanation: In root position C-E-G, 1st inversion is E-G-C, 2nd inversion is G-C-E.

questions.question: In the key of G major, what is the ii chord?
questions.explanation: ii in G major is built on the 2nd degree (A) and is minor: A-C-E → A minor.

questions.question: What is a sus4 chord?
options: A chord with the 3rd replaced by the 4th
questions.correct_answer: A chord with the 3rd replaced by the 4th
questions.explanation: Csus4 = C, F, G. The 3rd (E) is suspended and replaced by the 4th (F).

questions.question: What is the Nashville Number System's purpose?
options: Using numbers instead of chord names for easy transposition
questions.correct_answer: Using numbers instead of chord names for easy transposition
questions.explanation: The Nashville Number System assigns numbers to chords based on scale degree, making transposition easy.

questions.question: What makes a major 7th chord different from a dominant 7th?
options: Major 7th has a major 7th interval; dominant 7th has a minor 7th
questions.correct_answer: Major 7th has a major 7th interval; dominant 7th has a minor 7th
questions.explanation: Cmaj7 = C E G B (major 7th). C7 = C E G B♭ (minor 7th = dominant).

questions.question: What is the common pop chord progression I-V-vi-IV in C major?
questions.explanation: I=C, V=G, vi=Am, IV=F. This is one of the most common pop progressions.

questions.question: What is a power chord?
options: A chord with root and 5th only (no 3rd)
questions.correct_answer: A chord with root and 5th only (no 3rd)
questions.explanation: Power chords (e.g., C5) contain only the root and 5th. Common in rock music.

questions.question: In standard Roman numeral analysis, lowercase numerals represent:
questions.explanation: Uppercase = major (I, IV, V), lowercase = minor (ii, iii, vi). vii° = diminished.

questions.question: What is a secondary dominant?
options: The V/V chord — a dominant of a non-tonic chord
A chord borrowed from parallel minor
questions.correct_answer: The V/V chord — a dominant of a non-tonic chord
questions.explanation: V/V is the dominant of the dominant. In C major, V/V = D major (dominant of G).

questions.question: What is a Neapolitan chord?
options: A major chord built on the flattened 2nd degree
questions.correct_answer: A major chord built on the flattened 2nd degree
questions.explanation: The Neapolitan chord (♭II) is a major triad on the lowered 2nd scale degree, usually in first inversion.

questions.question: What notes make up an F major chord?
questions.explanation: F major: F (root), A (major 3rd), C (perfect 5th).

questions.question: What is a cadence?
options: A chord progression that creates a sense of resolution
questions.correct_answer: A chord progression that creates a sense of resolution
questions.explanation: A cadence is a harmonic goal, typically at the end of a phrase (e.g., V-I = authentic cadence).

questions.question: What is a plagal cadence?
questions.explanation: A plagal cadence (IV-I) is also known as the 'Amen cadence' heard in hymns.

questions.question: What is a half-diminished 7th chord?
questions.explanation: Half-diminished (ø7): diminished triad + minor 7th. Also written as m7♭5.

questions.question: What notes make up a D minor chord?
questions.explanation: D minor: D (root), F (minor 3rd), A (perfect 5th).
